hypothesis: If a conditional variational autoencoder trained on Li–Si–X crystal graphs is conditioned on >1500 mAh/g capacity and large, charge-state-invariant Li+ migration channels and its outputs are structurally relaxed with a machine-learned interatomic potential, then the resulting materials will be energetically stable silicon-based anode candidates that maintain large continuous Li+ diffusion pathways between charged and discharged states because the learned latent space encodes transferable structure–property relationships linking topology to capacity and channel robustness.
